title: What Redshift Tells Astronomers
script_summary: A concise explanation of cosmological redshift and its implications for understanding the expanding universe.

segments.voiceover: When galaxies look redder, space itself has stretched their light.
As the universe expands, light from distant galaxies shifts to longer wavelengths.
The further away a galaxy is, the more redshifted its light appears.
This helps astronomers measure the universe's expansion rate, revealing its vastness.
Want to explore more cosmic mysteries? Follow for more!

full_voiceover: When galaxies look redder, space itself has stretched their light. This phenomenon is called 'cosmological redshift'. As the universe expands, light from distant galaxies shifts to longer wavelengths. The further away a galaxy is, the more redshifted its light appears. This helps astronomers measure the universe's expansion rate, revealing its vastness. Want to explore more cosmic mysteries? Follow for more!
